message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Lord Fletcher's (Lord Fletcher Inn) (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 232 reviews (listed as CLOSED); Tripadvisor presence with legacy reviews; legacy Google footprint exists but current count not directly verified [UNVERIFIED exact Google count]
- Sentiment: Legacy: mostly positive, nostalgic — prime rib and Sinatra-era atmosphere consistently praised; some reviewers noted dated decor/food in final years. No current operating sentiment (closed since 2020).
- Longevity: Operated 1966-2020 (54 years) under Fletcher family (founder Ron Fletcher; passed to son Michael Fletcher). Closed during pandemic. Acquired by Life & Times Hospitality (Mike and Matt French) with reopening targeted for 2026.
- Credentials: ["Frank Sinatra documented regular for 30+ years (Coachella Valley Independent, multiple press)","Coachella Valley Independent feature: 'End of an Era' (2020)","Recognized as the original restaurant that launched Rancho Mirage's Restaurant Row (Palm Springs Life, local press)","NBC Palm Springs / Roggin Report coverage (2025)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for historical/nostalgia queries ('Sinatra restaurants Palm Springs', 'Rancho Mirage landmark restaurant', 'Lord Fletcher's history'). Does NOT surface in current 'best restaurants Rancho Mirage 2026', 'best steakhouse Rancho Mirage', or 'fine dining Rancho Mirage' lists — those are dominated by The Edge Steakhouse (Ritz-Carlton), The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente, Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare, and Foxy's Kitchen + Bar. Appears in 'English pub Palm Springs' queries but flagged as closed.
- Site structured data: Two parallel sites exist: legacy lordfletcher.com (SSL certificate verification failed on fetch) and new lordfletcherinn.com. The new site has no schema.org markup, no hours, no menu, no phone, no social links — only an email signup, address (70385 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270), and 'Opening 2026' tagline. Equestrian/polo brand imagery suggests a repositioning. Agent-readability is currently very low.
- Visibility gap score: 8
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"Lord Fletcher’s to Return with New Ownership","url":"https://whatnow.com/orange-county/restaurants/lord-fletchers-to-return-with-new-ownership/"},{"title":"English-themed Lord Fletcher’s pioneered Rancho Mirage fine dining","url":"https://acesgolf.com/english-themed-lord-fletchers-pioneered-rancho-mirage-fine-dining/"},{"title":"Lord fletcher's palm desert","url":"https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f3d99c8ac8326d8bba4f4b/66fa2a96efc2ea9ee115229b_47945374317.pdf"},{"title":"End of an Era: Remembering the Legendary Lord Fletcher's—a Favorite of Frank Sinatra, and a Classic Prime Rib Destination - Coachella Valley Independent","url":"https://cvindependent.com/2020/09/end-of-an-era-remembering-the-legendary-lord-fletcher-s-a-favorite-of-frank-sinatra-and-a-classic-prime-rib-destination/"},{"title":"Becoming a City - Welcome to Rancho Mirage","url":"https://rm50th.com/history/becoming_a_city.php"},{"title":"Lord Fletcher’s: Rancho Mirage Classic Dining Discovery | Peter Moruzzi's Mid-Century","url":"https://petermoruzzi.com/2013/03/10/lord-fletchers-rancho-mirage-classic-dining-discovery/"}]

I found several relevant sources about Lord Fletcher's. Here's what the search results show:

## Overview

Lord Fletcher's was a unique English-style restaurant that stood as a Coachella Valley landmark for more than 50 years. Founded by Ron Fletcher, the iconic restaurant originally opened in 1966, and was located at 70385 Highway 111 in Rancho Mirage.

## Frank Sinatra Connection

Frank Sinatra, who became a close friend of Fletcher and his son Michael, was perhaps the restaurant's best known and most loyal customer. The pub is where Frank Sinatra liked to dine, as did Lucille Ball. The Chairman of the Board, or Ol' Blue Eyes, had his regular seat in the bar—his signed photo now hangs over it.

## The "End of an Era"

For more than a half-century, Lord Fletcher's stood as a landmark on Highway 111—but in August 2020, the restaurant announced it would not reopen. Lord Fletcher's had always closed for summer, but COVID-19 meant the closure happened extra-early this year, in March.

## Recent Developments

However, the restaurant is making a comeback under new ownership. It was recently acquired by Life & Times Hospitality, a group that operates The Red Dog Saloon and Pioneertown Motel in Pioneertown, as well as The Copper Room in Yucca Valley.

Based on the search results, here's the history of Lord Fletcher's Inn in Rancho Mirage:

## History and Founding

Lord Fletcher's was opened in 1966 by Ron Fletcher as an English countryside inn-inspired restaurant with a fascinating collection of authentic art, antique swords and lances, tapestries, beer steins, and bric-a-brac — all selected by Fletcher and shipped from England. The restaurant launched "Restaurant Row" in Rancho Mirage, California.

## The Fletcher Family and Ownership

The congenial second generation restaurateur Michael Fletcher manages Lord Fletcher's. The restaurant remained under family ownership throughout its operation.

## Notable History and Celebrity Clientele

A portrait of Sir Winston Churchill greets guests and reflects the intimacy and familiarity of the restaurant felt by the guests, who included Rancho Mirage homeowners Lucille Ball, Walter Annenberg, Kirk Douglas, and Gerald Ford. Frank Sinatra, who became a close friend of Fletcher and his son Michael, was perhaps the restaurant's best known and most loyal customer.

## Closure and Recent Status

For more than a half-century, Lord Fletcher's has stood as a landmark on Highway 111. Lord Fletcher's had always closed for summer, but COVID-19 meant the closure happened extra-early this year, in March (2020), and in August it was learned that the restaurant would not reopen.

Lord Fletcher's Inn — a legendary dining club — was established in 1966 and is returning to Rancho Mirage, California, opening in 2026.

City officials say new owners are planning to reopen the restaurant and have recently received tenant improvement permits, though no official reopening date has been announced.

**Background:**
Lord Fletcher's, a unique English-style restaurant that stood as a Coachella Valley landmark for more than 50 years, is located at 70385 Highway 111 in Rancho Mirage. It unfortunately closed during the pandemic, and it was originally announced it would not reopen.

The reporting indicates there is active progress with the permit filings, but specific reopening timelines have not yet been announced.

confirms: Longevity: opened 1966 by Ron Fletcher, passed to son Michael Fletcher, closed during pandemic in 2020 (announced August 2020 it would not reopen) — confirmed by Coachella Valley Independent, aces golf article, Peter Moruzzi, and Rancho Mirage 50th history site.
Frank Sinatra documented regular — confirmed by multiple sources including CV Independent feature; signed photo hangs over his regular bar seat.
Coachella Valley Independent 'End of an Era' feature (September 2020) — confirmed at cvindependent.com.
Original launcher of Rancho Mirage 'Restaurant Row' / pioneered Rancho Mirage fine dining — confirmed by acesgolf.com and other local press.
NBC Palm Springs / Roggin Report coverage — confirmed: multiple Roggin Report articles dated Sept 2025 and Feb 2026 about reopening uncertainty and TI permits.

Acquired by Life & Times Hospitality, run by brothers Mike and Matt French (also operators of Pioneertown Motel, Red Dog Saloon, Copper Room) — confirmed via NBC Palm Springs / whatnow.com / Life & Times site.
Targeted reopening 2026 — confirmed by lordfletcherinn.com tagline and whatnow.com coverage, though Feb 2026 Roggin Report notes no official reopening date announced (permit stage).
flagged_as_unverified: Exact Yelp review count of 232 — not independently verified in this pass.
Tripadvisor presence with legacy reviews — not directly verified.
Sentiment characterization ('mostly positive, nostalgic; some noted dated decor/food in final years') — plausible and consistent with press but not independently sampled from review platforms.
Specific query-surface claims (e.g., dominance of The Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, etc., in 'best steakhouse Rancho Mirage 2026' lists) — not verified in this lens.

Palm Springs Life specifically citing Lord Fletcher's as the original Restaurant Row launcher — the claim is broadly corroborated by local press (acesgolf, Peter Moruzzi), but a Palm Springs Life attribution specifically was not located.
lordfletcher.com SSL failure — not tested in this lens.
Schema.org/agent-readability absence on lordfletcherinn.com — not verified in this lens.
Reopening date of 2026 is aspirational; Feb 2026 Roggin Report says permits only — no confirmed date.
input.notes: Core history (1966 founding, Fletcher family ownership, Sinatra clientele, 2020 closure, Life & Times acquisition by Mike/Matt French, 2026 reopening target) all independently confirmed across multiple primary and trade sources. No James Beard or Michelin credentials were claimed (correctly — none exist in JBF or Michelin databases). The 2026 reopening should be softened to 'targeted/permits underway' per Feb 2026 Roggin Report rather than confirmed.

content.text:
